headline: Cryptocurrency Losses Hit RM2.1B Among Malaysian Investors
context: Securities Commission Malaysia estimated that Malaysian retail investors lost RM2.1 billion in cryptocurrency investments during 2024-2025, with 78% of losses occurring on unregistered offshore platforms.

cards.big: RM2.1 billion lost — 420,000 Malaysians burned by crypto, and 78% traded on illegal platforms
cards.sub: SC has registered only 4 crypto exchanges but Malaysians accessed over 180 unregistered platforms

cards.big: Average crypto loss per affected investor was RM5,000 — equivalent to 3 months of B40 household income
cards.sub: Leveraged trading on Binance and Bybit accounted for 52% of losses despite both being unlicensed in Malaysia

cards.big: 72% of crypto investors aged 18-34 with 45% holding only diploma or SPM qualification
cards.sub: Social media influencers promoted unregistered tokens to 2.8 million followers without disclosure requirements

cards.big: SC issued 68 enforcement actions in 2025 but recovered only RM14 million — 0.7% of total investor losses
cards.sub: Cross-border jurisdiction makes it nearly impossible to pursue offshore platform operators or freeze assets

cards.big: Banning platforms doesn't work when every Malaysian with a phone can download an offshore crypto app in 30 seconds
Should Malaysia follow Singapore's model requiring all crypto platforms serving locals to register regardless of domicile?
